summary: Graham A. Colditz is an epidemiologist and public health expert focused on cancer prevention, implementation science, and eliminating health disparities. He emphasizes the translation of existing epidemiological data into actionable, tailored prevention strategies for populations and individuals.

principles.statement: We must actively apply the scientific knowledge we already possess to prevent cancer.
principles.rationale: The collective toll of cancer can be significantly reduced if we focus on implementing lifestyle changes and prevention strategies based on current epidemiological data.
principles.source_quote: Our challenge is to act on the knowledge we have. We need to stack the deck for prevention – embrace the opportunity to reduce our collective cancer toll by changing the way we live.

mental_models.name: Stacking the deck for prevention
mental_models.description: Treating disease prevention as a process of systematically improving the odds of healthy outcomes across a population by altering lifestyle factors and translating epidemiological data into actionable public health programs.
mental_models.example: Creating the "Your Disease Risk" website to provide tailored prevention messages and improve risk stratification for the public.

signature_quotes.text: Our challenge is to act on the knowledge we have. We need to stack the deck for prevention – embrace the opportunity to reduce our collective cancer toll by changing the way we live.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the opportunity to reduce cancer rates through lifestyle changes and prevention.
